RepurpOS

Parkinson's Disease

Disease intelligence for Parkinson Disease: 165 alterations and 540 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

US DALYs633.9KWHO GHE 2021 · all ages
Global DALYs6.89MWHO GHE 2021 · worldwide
US mortality38.8K deaths/yrWHO GHE 2021
Global mortality348.5K deaths/yrWHO GHE 2021 · worldwide
NIH funding$255MFY2025 · RCDC estimate
Funding levelModerate
Spontaneous remissionProgressive neurodegeneration — no spontaneous remission; untreated motor decline averages 3-5 UPDRS points/year…

Gap sizeLarge — DBS significantly underused; no disease-modifying therapy exists; current treatments are purely symptomatic
Primary barrierbiological + practice

Home/ RepurpOS/ Parkinson's Disease

Disease overview

Alterations
165
Molecular (A)
100
Clinical (B–E)

Direct drugs

Via biomarker

Natural products
58
Merged ranked
540
With trial/literature evidence
25

Sources: Open Targets, HPO, ClinicalTrials.gov, DGIdb, ChEMBL, PubMed Literature, PubMed NP, ClinicalTrials.gov NP · Pipeline phase 7 · Updated 2026-07-05

Remission & chronicity

Spontaneous remission
Progressive neurodegeneration — no spontaneous remission; untreated motor decline averages 3-5 UPDRS points/year; non-motor symptoms (depression, cognitive decline, autonomic dysfunction) also progressive
Best-intervention remission
Levodopa/carbidopa: 40-50% motor improvement in early disease but efficacy wanes (motor fluctuations in 50% at 5 years, dyskinesias in 30%); deep brain stimulation (DBS): 50-60% reduction in off-time, 60-70% reduction in dyskinesias at 5 years; LCIG (levodopa-carbidopa intestinal gel infusion): near-continuous on-time in advanced patients
Drug-free remission
—
Relapse after remission
—
Chronicity
—
Gap size
Large — DBS significantly underused; no disease-modifying therapy exists; current treatments are purely symptomatic
Primary barrier
biological + practice
Barrier detail: No neuroprotective or disease-modifying treatment is approved despite decades of research; DBS is offered too late in disease course (often 10+ years after diagnosis vs evidence for benefit in earlier disease — EARLYSTIM trial); focused ultrasound (FDA cleared 2021) underused as non-invasive alternative

Last SoC change: 2023 — FDA cleared focused ultrasound thalamotomy (FUS) for bilateral PD tremor; 2021 FDA cleared Parkinson's-specific wearable (KinetiGraph); levodopa has been the backbone since 1967 — one of the longest-unchanged standards in medicine

Remission data layers: disease_db_100 · source-locked manual data · confidence: high

Related: Intervention atlas

Identifiers: MONDO: MONDO:0005180 · ORPHA: ORPHA:411602

Right to Try Relevance

Eligibility: This disease meets the federal and state right-to-try legal standard (21 U.S.C. § 360bbb-0a and Montana SB 535): it lacks disease-modifying therapy with indication-specific FDA approval. Patients with Parkinson Disease may be eligible to access investigational therapies outside of clinical trials under right-to-try frameworks.
Investigational Agents
263
Phase 3: 46, Phase 2: 166, Phase 1: 51
Most Advanced Phase
Phase 3
Highest dev. stage
Gene Targets
165
Actionable
Pipeline Compounds (Top 6)
Darapladib, Fenretinide, Ambroxol, Amoxicillin, Ampreloxetine, Atomoxetine
Legal Standard: Right-to-try requires the disease lacks "adequate approved therapy for that specific disease" (not symptom-management drugs). This distinction means a disease with antidepressants available still qualifies if no FDA approval exists for THAT disease indication.
View Right-to-Try Compendium

DONEPEZIL Repurposing Small molecule Approved Strong 95
8 trials 1 Cochrane 2 Meta-analysis 4 Trial pubs 3 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT02012595 — [11C]Donepezil PET For Imaging The Autonomous Nervous System In Parkinsons Disease (COMPLETED)
  • NCT02206620 — Effects of Cholinergic Augmentation on Measures of Balance and Gait (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT03011476 — Effect of Acetylcholinesterase Inhibitors on the Gait of the Patients With Parkinson Disease (UNKNOWN) PHASE4
  • NCT00030979 — Donepezil to Treat Dementia in Parkinson's Disease (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT06903910 — Investigating the Cholinergic Contribution to Gait Dysfunction in Parkinson's Disease (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT01014858 — Multi-centre UK Study of the Acetylcholinesterase Inhibitor Donepezil in Early Dementia Associated With Parkinson's Disease (TERMINATED) PHASE3
  • NCT02857244 — A Multidisciplinary Approach to Manage Gait Difficulty in Parkinson Patients (WITHDRAWN) PHASE2
  • NCT01327859 — Safety, Tolerability, and Efficacy of Donepezil (Aricept) in Parkinson' s Disease (PD) Patients With Dementia (COMPLETED) PHASE3

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Practice Parameter: evaluation and treatment of depression, psychosis, and dementia in Parkinson disease (an evidence-based review): report of the Quality Standards Subcommittee of the American Academy of Neurology (Neurology, 2006)
  • Meta-analysis Cholinesterase Inhibitors for Delusions and Hallucinations in Alzheimer Disease and Parkinson Disease: Questionably Significant Benefits (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2023)
  • Meta-analysis Cholinesterase Inhibitors for Treatment of Psychotic Symptoms in Alzheimer Disease and Parkinson Disease: A Meta-analysis (JAMA neurology, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication [Dementia with Lewy bodies and Parkinson disease dementia, their diagnoses and treatment strategies] (Nihon yakurigaku zasshi. Folia pharmacologica Japonica, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Early-start vs delayed-start donepezil against cognitive decline in Parkinson disease: a randomized clinical trial (Expert opinion on pharmacotherapy, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of a central cholinesterase inhibitor on reducing falls in Parkinson disease (Neurology, 2010)
  • Clinical trial publication Efficacy and safety of donepezil in the treatment of executive dysfunction in Parkinson disease: a pilot study (Clinical neuropharmacology, 2005)

8 trials 1 Cochrane 2 Meta-analysis 4 Trial pubs 3 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT02012595 — [11C]Donepezil PET For Imaging The Autonomous Nervous System In Parkinsons Disease (COMPLETED)
  • NCT02206620 — Effects of Cholinergic Augmentation on Measures of Balance and Gait (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT03011476 — Effect of Acetylcholinesterase Inhibitors on the Gait of the Patients With Parkinson Disease (UNKNOWN) PHASE4
  • NCT00030979 — Donepezil to Treat Dementia in Parkinson's Disease (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT06903910 — Investigating the Cholinergic Contribution to Gait Dysfunction in Parkinson's Disease (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT01014858 — Multi-centre UK Study of the Acetylcholinesterase Inhibitor Donepezil in Early Dementia Associated With Parkinson's Disease (TERMINATED) PHASE3
  • NCT02857244 — A Multidisciplinary Approach to Manage Gait Difficulty in Parkinson Patients (WITHDRAWN) PHASE2
  • NCT01327859 — Safety, Tolerability, and Efficacy of Donepezil (Aricept) in Parkinson' s Disease (PD) Patients With Dementia (COMPLETED) PHASE3

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Practice Parameter: evaluation and treatment of depression, psychosis, and dementia in Parkinson disease (an evidence-based review): report of the Quality Standards Subcommittee of the American Academy of Neurology (Neurology, 2006)
  • Meta-analysis Cholinesterase Inhibitors for Delusions and Hallucinations in Alzheimer Disease and Parkinson Disease: Questionably Significant Benefits (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2023)
  • Meta-analysis Cholinesterase Inhibitors for Treatment of Psychotic Symptoms in Alzheimer Disease and Parkinson Disease: A Meta-analysis (JAMA neurology, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication [Dementia with Lewy bodies and Parkinson disease dementia, their diagnoses and treatment strategies] (Nihon yakurigaku zasshi. Folia pharmacologica Japonica, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Early-start vs delayed-start donepezil against cognitive decline in Parkinson disease: a randomized clinical trial (Expert opinion on pharmacotherapy, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of a central cholinesterase inhibitor on reducing falls in Parkinson disease (Neurology, 2010)
  • Clinical trial publication Efficacy and safety of donepezil in the treatment of executive dysfunction in Parkinson disease: a pilot study (Clinical neuropharmacology, 2005)

Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.
